title: IdentityIntakeInteraction
description: The `identity_intake` interaction kind — structured name/email/phone lead capture, the first instance of the Rich Interactions pattern. Render capability: `identity_form`. On capable channels the client renders an inline form from `Spec`; on text-only channels the server degrades to validated conversational turn-by-turn collection. Either way the turn resumes with the same validated `Payload`. Kind-specific shapes referenced by the generic `interaction_required` / `submit_interaction` envelope.

Spec.title: IdentityIntakeSpec
Spec.description: The `spec` carried on `interaction_required` for kind `identity_intake`: which fields to collect.

fields.description: The identity fields to collect, in display order.

required.description: Whether the visitor must provide this field to submit.

label.description: Optional display label overriding the client's default for this field.

Values.title: IdentityIntakeValues
Values.description: The `values` a client submits via `submit_interaction` for kind `identity_intake`. Validated server-side: required fields present, email shape, phone normalized to E.164.

phone.description: The visitor's phone number (normalized server-side to E.164).

Payload.title: IdentityIntakePayload
Payload.description: The canonical validated payload the parked turn resumes with (identical on the form and conversational paths). On success the server also attaches the identity to the session (metadata `userName` / `contactEmail` / `contactPhone` — the same keys the OTP contact seam reads).

values.description: Present when `status` is `submitted`: the validated, normalized values.

message.description: Guidance for the agent when `status` is `declined` / `no_response`.
